headline.purpose: Dialog titles, major panel titles, and high-level component headings.

title.purpose: Card titles, compact section headers, and repeated component titles.

body.purpose: Default component text, menu items, table cells, and descriptions.

label.purpose: Table headers, group labels, sidebar sections, and compact metadata.

shadows.name: hairline-depth
shadows.purpose: Default cards, inputs, and small framed surfaces.

shadows.name: floating-depth
shadows.purpose: Popovers, dropdown menus, autocomplete lists, toasts, and elevated cards.

motion.name: state-fast
motion.purpose: Button, menu, popover, and sidebar state transitions.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The main command style for one clear action per surface.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 36px; padding: 6px 14px; border: 0; border-radius: 6px; background: hsl(var(--primary, 275 90% 29%)); color: hsl(var(--primary-foreground, 0 0% 100%)); font: 500 14px/1.4 Inter, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; box-shadow: var(--shadow-sm-val, 0 1px 2px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.04)); transition: background-color 150ms ease-out, box-shadow 150ms ease-out, transform 150ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: hsl(var(--primary, 275 90% 29%) / 0.9); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid hsl(var(--ring, 275 90% 29%)); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); box-shadow: var(--shadow-xs-val, 0 1px 2px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.03)); }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: A neutral command style that lets purple appear only on interaction.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 36px; padding: 6px 14px; border: 1px solid hsl(var(--border, 220 13% 91%)); border-radius: 6px; background: hsl(var(--background, 220 14% 96%)); color: hsl(var(--foreground, 0 0% 20%)); font: 500 14px/1.4 Inter, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; transition: border-color 150ms ease-out, background-color 150ms ease-out, color 150ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { border-color: hsl(var(--primary, 275 90% 29%) / 0.4); background: hsl(var(--primary, 275 90% 29%) / 0.05); color: hsl(var(--primary, 275 90% 29%)); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid hsl(var(--ring, 275 90% 29%)); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Compact field style for research forms and filter surfaces.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; height: 36px; padding: 6px 12px; border: 1px solid hsl(var(--input, 220 13% 91%)); border-radius: 6px; background: hsl(var(--background, 220 14% 96%)); color: hsl(var(--foreground, 0 0% 20%)); font: 400 14px/1.5 Inter, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; box-shadow: var(--shadow-xs-val, 0 1px 2px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.03)); transition: box-shadow 200ms ease-out, border-color 200ms ease-out; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: hsl(var(--muted-foreground, 215 14% 46%)); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid hsl(var(--primary, 275 90% 29%)); outline-offset: 2px; box-shadow: var(--shadow-sm-val, 0 1px 2px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.04)); }

components.name: Default Card
components.description: A quiet paper surface for grouped controls and readable summaries.
components.css: .ds-card { max-width: 380px; padding: 20px; border: 1px solid hsl(var(--border, 220 13% 91%)); border-radius: 6px; background: hsl(var(--card, 0 0% 100%)); color: hsl(var(--card-foreground, 0 0% 20%)); box-shadow: var(--shadow-xs-val, 0 1px 2px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.03)); font-family: Inter, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-card h3 { margin: 0 0 6px; font-size: 16px; line-height: 1.25; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0; } .ds-card p { margin: 0; color: hsl(var(--muted-foreground, 215 14% 46%)); font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.5; }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: A compact semantic chip for workflow state without dashboard noise.
components.css: .ds-status-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; border-radius: 9999px; padding: 2px 10px; background: hsl(var(--info-tint, 207 88% 94%)); color: hsl(var(--info, 211 100% 50%)); font: 600 12px/1.25 Inter, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; letter-spacing: 0.025em; }

components.name: Compact Table
components.description: The default data surface for scan-heavy research workflows.
components.css: .ds-table { width: 100%; border-collapse: separate; border-spacing: 0; overflow: hidden; border: 1px solid hsl(var(--border, 220 13% 91%)); border-radius: 6px; background: hsl(var(--card, 0 0% 100%)); color: hsl(var(--foreground, 0 0% 20%)); font: 400 14px/1.5 Inter, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-table thead { background: hsl(var(--muted, 220 14% 96%) / 0.4); } .ds-table th { height: 36px; padding: 0 12px; text-align: left; color: hsl(var(--muted-foreground, 215 14% 46%)); font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.025em; } .ds-table td { padding: 10px 12px; border-top: 1px solid hsl(var(--border, 220 13% 91%) / 0.75); } .ds-table tbody tr:hover { background: hsl(var(--primary, 275 90% 29%) / 0.04); }

components.name: Popover Surface
components.description: A floating utility surface for selection, filters, and compact actions.
components.html:
Popover content

Content remains clickable and layered.
components.css: .ds-popover { width: 256px; padding: 16px; border: 1px solid hsl(var(--border, 220 13% 91%)); border-radius: 6px; background: hsl(var(--card, 0 0% 100%)); color: hsl(var(--card-foreground, 0 0% 20%)); box-shadow: var(--shadow-md-val, 0 2px 4px -1px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.06), 0 4px 8px -2px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1)); font-family: Inter, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-popover strong { display: block; margin-bottom: 4px; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; } .ds-popover p { margin: 0; color: hsl(var(--muted-foreground, 215 14% 46%)); font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.5; }

narrative.overview: Violet UI is a component registry for focused academic tools. It should feel like a well-built research instrument: precise, calm, and ready for repeated use by people who care more about their data and workflow than the interface itself. The system is restrained by default. NYU Purple carries identity and interaction affordance, but it must stay rare enough that content, form state, and tabular information remain the main event.
keyCharacteristics: Compact density with readable touch targets.
Flat-by-default surfaces with tokenized borders and quiet elevation.
Familiar Radix and shadcn interaction patterns.
Accessible keyboard, contrast, focus, and reduced-motion behavior.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: NYU Purple is the only brand accent. If more than 10% of a screen is purple, the screen is shouting.

rules.name: The Semantic Color Rule
rules.body: Success, warning, info, and destructive colors are reserved for state. Do not use them to decorate cards or make empty states more exciting.

rules.name: The No Theatrics Rule
rules.body: Do not introduce display serifs, gradient type, oversized hero text, or novelty type. This registry serves product UI, not a campaign page.

rules.name: The Border-First Rule
rules.body: Use border and tonal layering before shadow. If a static card needs shadow-lg, the hierarchy is wrong.

rules.name: The No Glass Rule
rules.body: Decorative blur and glassmorphism are forbidden. Overlays may dim the page, but they must not become visual effects.

dos: Do use NYU Purple for primary action, focus, active state, and selected state.
Do keep surfaces compact: 36px desktop form controls, 20px modal and card padding, 12px table cell padding.
Do pair every background token with its matching foreground token, especially in dark mode.
Do use borders and muted fills before reaching for heavy shadows.
Do preserve Radix keyboard behavior, accessible names, focus rings, and reduced-motion respect.
Do write registry primitives that stay business-agnostic. Access requests, PI logic, room logic, and approval semantics belong in consuming apps.
donts: Don't build generic Bootstrap sites. The system must not feel like a default admin template.
Don't use emoji-heavy startup SaaS language or visual treatment.
Don't create dense enterprise dashboards like SAP or Jira. Density is allowed; visual clutter is not.
Don't make the UI feel corporate or casual. It should remain academic, focused, and refined.
Don't use colored side-stripe borders greater than 1px on cards, rows, callouts, or alerts.
Don't use decorative gradients, gradient text, glassmorphism, blur-heavy overlays, bouncy motion, or hover lift as default style.
Don't introduce access-request-specific fields, states, or workflow language into violet-ui components.
